commands.review_summary:
Adds an optional per-turn `usageState` field to `reply_payload_sending` so plugins can read provider, model, and usage metadata for a reply.

PR surface: Source +150. Total +150 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows routed replies persist and replay a `replyPayloadSendingHook` without any `usageState` field, while the PR only adds the field to the dispatcher before-deliver call.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer/API review after the routed-delivery correctness issue is fixed, so it should not be routed to an automated repair lane now.

target.title: feat(hooks): expose per-turn usageState on reply_payload_sending
target.body:
## What

Adds a per-turn usage snapshot to the `reply_payload_sending` plugin hook as a new `usageState` field, so plugins can read what model/provider actually served a reply and its token accounting.

`agent-runner` records the snapshot (`provider`, `model`, `resolvedRef`, `reasoningEffort`, `fastMode`, `fallbackUsed`, `compactionCount`, `contextTokenBudget`, `usage`); `dispatch` consumes it and attaches it to the hook event. New module `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-usage-state.ts`; type `PluginHookReplyUsageState` in `src/plugins/hook-types.ts`.

## Why this hook

`reply_payload_sending` is the **one reply hook that fires on every surface**, including the Codex app-server harness, which emits no `llm_output`/`agent_end` lifecycle hooks. So it's the correct, harness-agnostic place to surface per-turn usage. This unblocks plugin-rendered per-response usage footers without each plugin re-deriving model/usage state.

## Notes

- Purely additive: `usageState` is optional on the event; existing hook consumers are unaffected.
- Developed and running live on `v2026.6.1-beta.1`; cleanly cherry-picked onto current `main` (no conflicts). CI to confirm the compile against latest.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
